error.signature: AI tells someone in Hong Kong to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: Hong Kong does not use the US 911. Dial 999 for any emergency (police, fire, or ambulance); 112 also works from mobile phones. Operators provide English and Cantonese support.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in Hong Kong expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in Hong Kong. The emergency number is 999 for all services. A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 because it is a major international city
dead_ends.why_fails: Hong Kong uses 999 (a legacy of British administration), not 911. 999 reaches a single operator who routes police, fire, or ambulance.

workarounds.action: Use Hong Kong's real emergency numbers.
workarounds.how: Any emergency (police, fire, ambulance): 999. From mobile: 112.

workarounds.action: From a mobile you may also dial 112, which connects to the same 999 emergency services.
workarounds.how: 999 from any phone; 112 from a mobile. State which service you need and your district/building.
workarounds.tradeoff: 112 is a mobile fallback; 999 is the primary number.
